data.title: OpenAI teams up with consulting firms to sell AI coding agent Codex
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) is working with consulting firms, including Accenture (ACN), Capgemini (CAPMF) (CGEMY), and PricewaterhouseCoopers, to help sell its AI coding agent Codex to businesses.

Other consulting firms are CGI (GIB), Cognizant (CTSH), Infosys (INFY), and Tata Consultancy. OpenAI said these partners will help enterprise customers identify and deploy high-value Codex use cases across the software development lifecycle, moving from pilots to production-ready deployments.

The AI tech giant added that these firms are also using Codex internally, changing the way their teams operate to build repeatable ways to bring Codex to customers.

“Our professionals are using Codex to move from static requirements to working solutions in hours, not weeks. It’s enabling rapid prototyping, real-time workflow redesign, and faster iteration across the development lifecycle," said Lan Guan, chief AI officer, Accenture.

In addition, OpenAI said it reached four million weekly active Codex users, up from three million two weeks ago. Last week, OpenAI provided a major update to Codex.

OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman said in a post on X that the company will reset rate limits today.

The company added that it is launching Codex Labs, which brings OpenAI experts directly into organizations to help teams put Codex to work on real problems. Through hands-on workshops and working sessions, enterprise organizations learn where Codex fits, how to integrate it into existing workflows, and how to move from early usage to repeatable deployment, according to the company.

OpenAI said Virgin Atlantic is using Codex to increase test coverage and increase team velocity — reducing technical debt and improving performance. Meanwhile, Cisco is using it to understand and reason across large, interconnected repositories, the company added.

"Enterprise AI adoption requires much more than access to great tools. It demands deep expertise in change management, governance, and delivery at scale. PwC is excited to be recognized as a Codex Transformation Partner, reflecting our commitment to investing in dedicated Codex expertise and building repeatable approaches that help our clients move from pilot to production faster," said Matt Hobbs, Cloud, Engineering, Data and AI Lead, at PwC, in an email to Seeking Alpha.

The new Codex consulting program is part of the AI giant's push to focus on coding and enterprise businesses as it cuts back on other endeavors.

OpenAI faces tough competition from rival Anthropic (ANTHRO) in the AI services market for enterprises. Anthropic has become a dominant AI provider for businesses, in part due to the success of its coding and AI agent products.

OpenAI Chief Revenue Officer Denise Dresser said in an interview that its Codex consulting partners will help the company expand its reach to more potential enterprise customers than it could alone, the Wall Street Journal reported.

"Helping companies bridge that gap between how to use it, how to expand it and how to move even more quickly is part of our responsibility, and these partnerships are going to allow us to help scale that to the world," said Dresser.

OpenAI did not specify the financial terms of the partnership program, according to the report.

OpenAI said on Tuesday that it has hired Colleen Kapase as its new vice president of global partnerships and ecosystem.  Kapase will report to Dresser. Kapase, who joins from Google’s cloud unit, will oversee OpenAI’s Codex consulting partners, the report noted.

Besides using Codex to help customers with tasks like updating code, OpenAI's consulting partners will help bring Codex "into every single line of business," said Dresser. That includes areas of knowledge work like marketing, finance, and sales, where coding agents and tools are not currently as prevalent, the report added.

Dresser said Codex and Frontier are aimed at helping automate aspects of all knowledge work.

In February, Microsoft-backed (MSFT) OpenAI announced a deeper push into the lucrative enterprise market with the launch of Frontier, a platform that helps enterprises build, deploy, and manage AI agents. The company signed multi-year partnerships with Accenture, Boston Consulting Group, Capgemini, and McKinsey & Company to help deploy Frontier.

As an example of how Codex can be used outside of software development, Dresser said she has a Codex-built AI agent called "Chief," which summarizes meetings, writes notes, and can update Salesforce and various Slack channels.

OpenAI’s Codex consulting partners will also get access to the AI coding tool under the program. OpenAI already has partnerships with consulting firms such as Bain & Co. and PwC to help sell and use ChatGPT Enterprise, the report noted.

Both Anthropic and OpenAI have used consulting firms to help spread the use of AI and agents across businesses. Anthropic, which has not publicly shared how many people are using Claude Code, said in February that the number of weekly active users for its AI coding assistant had doubled since Jan. 1.

Dresser said OpenAI is also working with its Codex consulting partners to build up Codex Labs, an initiative that brings “hands-on workshops” to businesses to help them start using the coding tool, the report added.

Capgemini and PricewaterhouseCoopers did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

Update: The story was updated with PwC's statement and Sam Altman's post.

data.title: Astra to seek accelerated nod for Ultomiris in IgA nephropathy after positive late-stage data
data.description:
AstraZeneca (AZN) announced plans to seek accelerated approval for its rare disease therapy, Ultomiris, on Tuesday after the C5 complement inhibitor reached a key goal in a late-stage trial for immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN), a kidney disease.

Citing interim data from its I CAN Phase III trial, the Anglo-Swedish drugmaker said Ultomiris reduced proteinuria with a statistically significant and clinically meaningful effect at Week 34 in adults with IgAN.

A rare inflammatory disease, patients with IgAN show various immune-related signs and symptoms, including proteinuria, the presence of excessive amounts of protein in the urine.

The global trial, designed to enroll nearly 510 patients with IgAN who are at risk of disease progression, tested Ultomiris as a late-line option. The 34-week reduction of proteinuria, measured as a 24-hour urine protein creatinine ratio ((UPCR)), was one of its primary endpoints.

There were no new safety signals, and the intravenous therapy indicated a tolerability profile consistent with prior data, the company said.

I CAN is set to continue until data for its other primary endpoint related to estimated glomerular filtration rate are analyzed at Week 106.

Ultomiris is already approved in the U.S. and EU for rare diseases such as paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria and atypical hemolytic uremic syndrome.

“We look forward to filing these data with regulatory authorities in key regions while, in parallel, advancing this Phase III trial towards completion,” said Marc Dunoyer, CEO of Astra’s (AZN) rare disease unit Alexion.

data.title: Agnc signals 15%-17% returns at ~150 bp MBS spreads as April tangible book value rises ~6%
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AGNC Investment Corp. (AGNC) Q1 2026

Management view

  • “AGNC's economic return in the first quarter was negative 1.6%,” driven by a late-quarter risk-off move after “uncertainty associated with the war in Iran and the potential for a more widespread conflict in the Middle East caused interest rate volatility to increase, investor sentiment to turn negative and Agency MBS spreads to widen significantly” (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Peter Federico).

  • “At current spread levels, the return profile on Agency MBS is more attractive,” and management pointed to the spread backdrop changing from “135 basis points” at the Q4 call to a range “between 150 and 175 basis points” in the last two months, adding: “We believe Agency MBS in this spread range represent compelling value on both an absolute and relative basis” (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

  • “For the first quarter, AGNC reported a comprehensive loss of $0.18 per common share,” and “our economic return on tangible common equity was negative 1.6% for the quarter, consisting of $0.36 of dividends declared per common share and a $0.50 decrease in tangible net book value per share” (Executive VP & CFO Bernice Bell).

  • “As of late last week, our tangible net book value per common share was up approximately 6% for April or 5% net of our monthly dividend accrual,” and “with the recovery in April through the end of last week, our tangible net book value has now largely reversed the first quarter decline” (Executive VP & CFO Bell).

  • Portfolio actions emphasized lower coupons and more swap hedging: “The market value of our portfolio totaled $95 billion at quarter end,” “we purchased $1.7 billion of predominantly low coupon specified pools,” and “the weighted average coupon on our portfolio declined to 4.95% from 5.12% in the prior quarter” (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

Outlook

  • Federico framed the near-term as volatile but with technical and policy-related potential catalysts: “although the sharp increase in geopolitical and macroeconomic risk creates a more challenging investment environment over the near term, the return profile and technical backdrop for agency mortgage-backed securities improved in the first quarter,” and “actions by the administration to improve housing affordability are more likely” (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

  • On supply/demand setup, management said: “With mortgage rates now about 50 basis points higher, MBS supply could be $50 billion to $70 billion lower this year,” and “money manager demand for MBS increased materially in the first quarter as bond fund inflows came in about double the pace of the previous 2 years” (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

  • No explicit EPS, revenue, or dividend guidance was provided in the Q1 2026 transcript, so comparisons to analyst estimates were not applicable.

Financial results

  • Bell highlighted earnings power from carry: “Net spread and dollar roll income was $0.42 per common share for the quarter, up $0.07 from the fourth quarter,” adding that “the increase was largely due to a 25 basis point increase in our net interest spread” (Executive VP & CFO Bell).

  • Balance sheet and liquidity positioning: “We ended the first quarter with leverage of 7.4x tangible equity, up slightly from 7.2x as of Q4,” and “we also ended the quarter with a significant liquidity position of $7 billion of unencumbered cash and Agency MBS, representing 60% of tangible equity” (Executive VP & CFO Bell).

  • Prepayment metrics moved higher: “The average projected life CPR of our portfolio increased 70 basis points to 10.3%,” and “actual CPRs averaged 13.2% for the quarter compared to 9.7% in the prior quarter” (Executive VP & CFO Bell).

  • Capital management: “During the first quarter, we issued $401 million of common equity through our At-the-Market offering program at a significant premium to tangible net book value per share” (Executive VP & CFO Bell).

Q&A

  • Bose George, Keefe, Bruyette, & Woods, Inc.: asked if returns looked comparable from Q4-end to now and what ROE implies; President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico answered that spreads moved from ~150 bps to ~135 bps after the GSE purchase announcement and “now we're right back to where we were this morning,” adding: “at that level... returns are kind of in the -- broadly in the 15% to 17% range, centered right around 16%.”

  • Crispin Love, Piper Sandler & Co.: questioned sustainability of strong net spread/dollar roll and whether core earnings compress toward the dividend; President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico said margin rose “25 basis points to 2.06%,” called it “above the long-run economics,” and guided to “high 30s and low 40s” for net spread/dollar roll “over the relatively near term, several quarters.”

  • Ameeta Lobo Nelson, UBS Investment Bank: asked about “optimal leverage” amid recurring volatility; President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico emphasized liquidity to avoid forced deleveraging and said the company can “operate now... with a very reasonable leverage position and still generate excellent returns for shareholders.”

  • Harsh Hemnani, Green Street Advisors: asked about Q1 equity raise timing vs. prior “opportunistic” framing; President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico said issuance was faster than expected due to volatility, and described it as “accretive from a book value perspective” and “significantly accretive from an earnings perspective” given deployment returns “around 16” vs. stock dividend yield “around 13.5%.”

Sentiment analysis

  • Analysts: slightly negative to neutral, with questions centered on volatility, leverage decisions, sustainability of earnings/carry, hedging posture, and equity issuance timing, including prompts like “how do you think about optimal leverage... where near-term volatility keeps remaining a recurring feature?” (Ameeta Lobo Nelson, UBS Investment Bank) and whether core earnings “compress a little bit closer to the dividend” (Crispin Love, Piper Sandler & Co.).

  • Management: slightly positive but risk-aware, repeatedly balancing near-term uncertainty with improved setup, including “the sharp increase in geopolitical and macroeconomic risk creates a more challenging investment environment over the near term” alongside “these conditions support our favorable outlook for agency mortgage-backed securities” (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

  • Versus Q4 2025: management tone shifted from celebratory (“2025 was an exceptional year for AGNC shareholders”) to more defensive around geopolitics and spread volatility, while still pointing to policy, funding-market, and valuation support for Agency MBS (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

Quarter-over-quarter comparison

  • Q4 emphasized strong returns and spread tightening, including “comprehensive income of $0.89 per common share” and “economic return... 11.6%,” while Q1 shifted to spread widening and “a comprehensive loss of $0.18 per common share” with “economic return... negative 1.6%” (Executive VP & CFO Bell).

  • Hedging and positioning continued to tilt toward swaps, with swap hedge allocation rising from “70%” (Q4) to “78%” (Q1) in duration-dollar terms, alongside an explicit note that AGNC “reduced our exposure to treasury-based hedges” (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

  • Analysts’ focus rotated from Q4’s dividend coverage/spread-tightening regime questions toward Q1’s sustainability of elevated carry, leverage discipline under volatility, and the timing/role of capital issuance.

Risks and concerns

  • Federico tied the quarter’s dislocation directly to geopolitics: “uncertainty associated with the war in Iran... caused interest rate volatility to increase” and widened MBS spreads (President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

  • Prepayment risk and model uncertainty were highlighted through higher CPRs, with Bell noting model updates drove part of the projected-life CPR increase, and management also stressed ongoing need to manage prepayments via specified pools and maintaining “a positive duration gap” as “additional prepayment protection in a down rate scenario” (Executive VP & CFO Bell; President, CEO & Director and Chief Investment Officer Federico).

Final takeaway

AGNC described Q1 2026 results as dominated by a February-to-March reversal in volatility and mortgage spreads, producing a (-1.6%) economic return and a ($0.18) comprehensive loss per share, while pointing to an April book value rebound of ~6% and a setup it characterized as more attractive at wider spreads. Management emphasized liquidity ($7B unencumbered), modestly higher leverage (7.4x), increased carry ($0.42 net spread and dollar roll income), a greater swap-hedge mix, and continued opportunistic capital issuance when trading at a premium, while repeatedly framing geopolitical outcomes and policy actions (GSE activity, bank capital rules, repo market functionality) as key swing factors for spreads and returns.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Welltower trades lower after Land & Buildings issues cautious report
data.description:
Welltower (WELL) stock was falling after Land & Buildings Investment Management issued its investment opinion on the healthcare REIT.

Shares were 2.68% down to $203.84 after the market opened on Tuesday.

The investment manager specializing in publicly traded real estate issued a white paper that talks about why the company's ten-year executive program, launched on October 26, 2025, is likely to lead to inferior shareholder returns.

"This program is a mechanism for transferring enormous wealth from shareholders to management, and that investors should consider selling Welltower shares," said the white paper.

data.title: Wall Street tumbles amid renewed hopes of a U.S.-Iran truce, Warsh's confirmation hearing
data.description:
U.S. stocks seesawed on Tuesday as investors hoped for a peace deal between Iran and the U.S., while former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh started his confirmation hearings in the Senate.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.3%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.1%.

Over in the bond market, yields were higher. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 6 basis points higher at 4.31%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 6 basis points to 3.79%.

U.S. President Donald Trump told CNBC that he expects the U.S. to “end up with a great deal” with Iran to end the war. He also said he does not expect to extend the ongoing ceasefire, which will expire on Wednesday. “I think they have no choice,” he said.

Vice President JD Vance, along with the U.S. delegation, is expected to land in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Wednesday for another round of talks, CNN reported.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were slightly higher at $88, while Brent (CO1:COM) also rose to $96 per barrel.

Kevin Warsh, the former Fed governor whom President Donald Trump nominated as Federal Reserve chair, will face a barrage of questions on Tuesday during his confirmation hearings in the Senate. The confirmation hearing starts at 10 a.m. ET.

On the U.S. economy, retail sales jumped more than expected in March.

Pending home sales jumped 1.5% month-over-month in March, higher than consensus, the National Association of Realtors said. Moreover, February business inventories rose +0.4% MoM to $2,686.8B vs. +0.2% consensus, according to the U.S. Census Bureau.

data.title: Intel upgraded by HSBC as firm heaps praise on server CPU boost
data.description:
Intel (INTC) was upgraded by HSBC on Tuesday as the investment firm heaped praise on the continued demand for server CPUs, calling it a “bigger catalyst” for the semiconductor giant than its foundry push.

“Amidst overall foundry capacity constraints, Intel is reallocating its own foundry capacity, especially in Intel 3 and 7 from client to server CPUs,” analyst Frank Lee wrote in a note to clients. “This should enable 2026e server CPU shipments to grow 20% y-o-y. In a high-demand and supply-constrained environment, we expect Intel to charge a hefty pricing premium, driving [average selling prices] higher by 20% y-o-y. We expect the server CPU shortage to extend into 2027, which should drive server CPU shipments growth of 20% y-o-y in 2027e along with another 10% [average selling prices] hike.”

Lee, who raised his rating on Intel to Buy from Hold (and raised his price target to $95 from $50), said he now expects upside to Intel's server CPU business to start in the second-quarter, as he expects the company's revenue to be $14.2B, 9% above the Wall Street estimate. Further boosting Intel's results could be price hikes that aid its gross margins, Lee added.

Separately, on Tuesday, Intel was also upgraded by BNP Paribas. The company is set to report first-quarter results after the close of trading on April 23.

data.title: How the U.S.-Iran war benefits the materials sector – analyst
data.description:
The electric vehicle supply chain is rapidly transforming into a strategic and politicized sector similar to oil and gas, according to Adam Dawes, an analyst at Shaw and Partners.

In an interview with CNBC, Dawes pointed to China’s battery giant CATL investing $4.4B in its mining subsidiary as a significant signal of this shift.

The move by CATL reflects a broader trend toward vertical integration as battery manufacturers seek to avoid the volatility of spot commodity markets. Dawes explained that companies are increasingly pursuing longer-term offtake agreements rather than risking exposure to fluctuating prices.

Battery manufacturers, he noted, are no longer willing to absorb the uncertainties posed by volatile commodity markets and geopolitical tensions.

This responsibility for securing supply chains has shifted dramatically from governments to private corporations.

“Now companies are going out themselves and actually making that happen,” Dawes observed, adding that firms are looking at upstream allocations and potentially taking over mining sites directly to ensure they can access the materials needed for EV production.

Major miners like Rio Tinto (RIO) and BHP (BHP) are also adapting by pivoting away from their traditional reliance on iron ore toward “future-facing metals” such as copper and lithium.

Rio Tinto’s (RIO) copper production increased 9% to approximately 230,000 tons, demonstrating this strategic reorientation toward metals essential for the evolving energy sector (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP).

The ongoing Iran war has contributed to market volatility, but Dawes views this as ultimately positive for the materials sector. High volatility leads to higher costs, which eventually translates to elevated commodity prices.

For investors, Dawes recommends maintaining a steady hand and focusing on economic cycles rather than reacting to daily headlines. While geopolitical tensions may trigger knee-jerk reactions in the market, those who hold the line stand to benefit from the long-term structural shifts reshaping the materials sector.

Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), and (RSPM).

Energy ETFs: (XLE), (AMLP), (VDE), (XOP), (OIH), and (IXC).

Green Energy: (ICLN), (TAN), (FAN), (GRID), (QCLN), and (ERTH).

Electric vehicle ETFs: (DRIV), (KARS), (FDRV)

Copper and Copper Mining ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), and (JJCTF).

data.title: Astera Labs surges after Amazon's $100B Anthropic deal; UBS initiates at Neutral
data.description:
Astera Labs (ALAB) jumped 10% during Tuesday morning market action following the news that key customer Amazon (AMZN) had inked a $100B deal with Anthropic (ANTHRO). 

UBS initiated coverage of the AI networking supplier with a Neutral rating and a $180 price target.

"Since launching retimer products in C2019, ALAB has defended share with Amazon and made steps to broaden adoption across additional hyperscalers, validating both product quality and execution," said UBS analysts, led by Natalia Winkler, in an investor report. "Based on our bottom-up retimer unit estimates and expert conversations, we are constructive on ALAB's growth prospects over the next 2–3 years, with C2027 revenues 4% above consensus, driven primarily by retimers and its scale-up PCIe/UA Link switching portfolio."

However, Astera Labs' entry into an increasingly competitive switching market and a high valuation prompted the Neutral rating by UBS.

"The next phase—entry into PCIe switching, which started in 2Q:25 — introduces materially higher competitive risk," Winkler noted. "While the PCIe switch market is ~4x larger than retimers, it also has strong incumbent players like Broadcom (AVGO) and Microchip Technology (MCHP) accounting for 50%+ share and a new entrant in Marvell (MRVL). We model Astera Labs reaching ~25% share (from <5% current share) by the end of the decade but note it will require sustained execution against competition."

data.title: Vaalco Energy surges after strong initial results from new well offshore Gabon
data.description:
Vaalco Energy (EGY) up 13.3% in early trading Tuesday after saying it achieved strong initial results from its latest Etame field development well, part of its Gabon drilling campaign, while also remaining on track to restart production at its Baobab field in Côte d’Ivoire in Q2.

Vaalco (EGY) said the Etame 14H well was drilled, completed, and brought on stream, delivering an initial flow rate of about 4,850 gross bbl/day of oil, or 2,850 bbl/day net to the company, which is the operator with a 58.8% working interest.

The well encountered 325 meters of net pay in high-quality Gamba reservoir sands with better-than-expected porosity and permeability, the company said.

Vaalco (EGY) also said it had mobilized its rig to the nearby Ebouri platform and began drilling on its EEBOM-5H development well.

Beyond Gabon, Vaalco (EGY) said it has successfully towed its Baobab floating production, storage, and offloading vessel from Dubai to Côte d'Ivoire following a lengthy refurbishment.

The FPSO is now moored in its original location and should restart crude production from the 4,500 bbl/day field in Q2, the company said.

data.title: UNH surge puts spotlight on ETFs with the biggest exposure to the stock
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group (UNH), the sector's bellwether and the first major insurer to report this earnings season, surged on Tuesday after posting better-than-expected first-quarter results and raising its annual earnings outlook.

The jump in UNH share price has put investors’ focus on ETFs that have significant exposure to the health care giant’s stock.

Shares of the Minnesota-based company, which is considered a Hold by Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings, jumped nearly 10% to $354.35 in the morning.

Below are some of the ETFs with the largest portfolio allocations to UNH:

iShares U.S. Healthcare Providers ETF (IHF), 21.97% allocation

Roundhill UNH WeeklyPay ETF (UNHW), 20.56% allocation

T. Rowe Price Health Care ETF (TMED), 6.79% allocation

Invesco Dow Jones Industrial Average Dividend ETF (DJD), 5.29% allocation

FT Vest DJIA Dogs 10 Target Income ETF (DOGG), 4.96% allocation

Pathfinder Focused Opportunities ETF (PFOE), 4.64% allocation

Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV), 4.61% allocation

Eagle Capital Select Equity ETF (EAGL), 4.60% allocation

iShares U.S. Healthcare ETF (IYH), 4.40% allocation

Vanguard Health Care ETF (VHT), 3.94% allocation

Other healthcare-related ETFs: (IHI), (IXJ), (HRTS), and (FXH).
